name: DATA I/O CORP
business: Data I/O designs, manufactures and sells programming and security deployment systems and services for electronic device manufacturers. The company provides solutions for programming flash memory, microcontrollers, and secure elements. Its customers include OEMs, EMS providers, and programming centers in automotive, IoT, and industrial markets.

exposures.sentence: Data I/O designs, manufactures and sells programming and security deployment systems and services for electronic device manufacturers.
Data I/O Corporation is a global leader in data programming and provisioning solutions for flash memory, microcontrollers and security integrated circuits.

Our solutions, some of which include security deployment and process control capabilities, enable us to address the demanding requirements of the electronic device market, where applications security and IP protection are essential to our customer s success.
Automotive electronics represented approximately 64% of 2025 bookings, compared to 59% in 2024.
The buildout of Edge AI represents a significant emerging growth driver for the Company.
Demand for capital equipment was further impacted by a reallocation of technology spending, with AI and AI datacenter-related investments taking priority.
Our device programming solutions currently target two high volume growing markets: automotive electronics and IoT, including industrial, consumer electronics and wireless.
During 2025, the Company deployed artificial intelligence internally across all functional departments to accelerate operations and reduce costs.

catalysts.event: Launch of LumenX2 programming platform at productronica
catalysts.sentence: The Company launched the next-generation LumenX2 programming platform and LumenX2-M4 manual programmer at the productronica 2025 tradeshow in Munich, Germany in November 2025.
The Acumatica implementation project remains on schedule with a target switchover date of July 1, 2026.

Subsequent to year end, in February 2026, the Company announced a collaboration with IAR, a leader in embedded development tools and security solutions, to combine IAR s security expertise with Data I/O s provisioning expertise.

catalysts.event: Filed shelf registration statement on Form S-3
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, the Company filed a shelf registration statement on Form S-3 providing the ability to issue up to $20 million dollars of equity securities.
By September 2025, the incident had been completely contained and remediated, all global systems were restored.

catalysts.event: Additional $1M annual run rate savings to be realized
catalysts.sentence: The Company has identified plans for an additional $1.0 million of annual run rate savings to be realized within the first half of 2026.
